name: Ascent to Cavall Bernat — circular through the Paraje Natural de la Murta
name_original: Subida al Cavall Bernat — circular por el Paraje Natural de la Murta
region_intro: Urban hiking trails in Paratge Natural Municipal La Murta traverse a diverse landscape characterized by Mediterranean pine forests, oak groves, and aromatic flora. The terrain includes fertile valleys flanked by abrupt mountains, with prominent peaks offering expansive views. Natural springs and streams are present throughout the area. Best urban hiking trails in Paratge Natural Municipal La Murta The most popular urban hiking route is Way of the Cross – View of Cullera loop from Cullera, an easy 2.2 miles (3.6 km) trail that takes 1 hour to complete. This route offers views of Cullera and features a moderate elevation gain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Walls of Alzira loop from Alzira, a moderate 9.1 miles (14.6 km) path. This route explores the historical walls of Alzira, featuring gentle ascents and varied urban scenery. Local hikers also love the Hiking loop from Alzira, a 6.4 miles (10.3 km) trail leading through urban and natural areas, often completed in about 2 hours 36 minutes. Hiking in Paratge Natural Municipal La Murta is defined by Mediterranean forests, historical ruins, and varied urban and natural paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to moderate ascents. The routes in Paratge Natural Municipal La Murta are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 190 reviews. More than 1000 hikers have used komoot to explore Paratge Natural Municipal La Murta's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many urban hiking trails are available in Paratge Natural Municipal La Murta?
A: There are 6 urban hiking trails available in Paratge Natural Municipal La Murta. These routes range from easy to moderate difficulty, offering options for various fitness levels.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about urban hiking in Paratge Natural Municipal La Murta?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from 192 ratings. Hikers often praise the diverse landscapes, the historical ruins of the Monastery, and the well-marked paths that allow for a peaceful escape into nature, even within an urban-adjacent setting.

Q: Are there any easy urban hiking routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, for an easier urban hike, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31703616" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Way of the Cross – View of Cullera loop from Cullera&lt;/a&gt;. This route is approximately 3.6 kilometers long with an elevation gain of about 81 meters, making it a great option for a leisurely stroll or a family outing. The Senda Histórica (SL-CV 82) mentioned in the region research is also an easy and accessible route, ideal for families, leading to the monastery ruins.

Q: What historical landmarks can I discover along the urban hiking trails?
A: Urban hiking in Paratge Natural Municipal La Murta offers a chance to explore significant historical sites. A prominent attraction is the 14th-century ruins of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6304841" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Santa María de la Murta&lt;/a&gt;, which includes ancient ponds and an aqueduct. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5346222" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Murta Icehouse&lt;/a&gt;, an old nevero, adding to the historical charm of the area.

Q: Are there any circular urban walking routes in the area?
A: Yes, several urban hiking routes in Paratge Natural Municipal La Murta are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/21935479" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hiking loop from Alzira&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 10.3 km circular route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40357508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaça de la Verge Fountain – The ruins loop from Cullera&lt;/a&gt; offers a 6.4 km moderate loop with scenic views.

Q: What are the best viewpoints or scenic spots on the urban trails?
A: Many trails offer rewarding views. Peaks like Cavall Bernat, La Creu del Cardenal, and L'Ouet provide breathtaking panoramic vistas that can extend to the Mediterranean coastline. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/362227" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Creu del Cardenal&lt;/a&gt; is a notable summit offering excellent perspectives.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the urban hiking trails in Paratge Natural Municipal La Murta?
A: While the region is generally welcoming to outdoor activities, specific regulations for dogs within the Municipal Natural Park can vary. It's always best to check local signage or the park's official guidelines regarding pet access to ensure a pleasant and compliant experience for you and your dog.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the urban hikes?
A: The terrain in Paratge Natural Municipal La Murta is diverse, featuring dense Mediterranean forests, abrupt mountains, and fertile valleys. You'll encounter well-marked paths, some with moderate elevation changes, and the possibility of karstification creating caves and chasms. The Senda Botánica (SL-CV 81) offers a chance to appreciate the park's rich flora.

Q: Is public transport available to access the urban hiking trails?
A: The region is accessible, but direct public transport to specific trailheads within the natural park might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or consider driving to nearby towns like Alzira or Cullera, from which some routes originate, and then proceeding to the trail starting points.

Q: Are there any urban hikes that focus on the natural flora and fauna?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4462728" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Murta Botanical Trail&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to showcase the park's rich botanical diversity. The unique microclimate supports nearly 1,000 species of phanerogams and numerous endemic flora. You might also encounter diverse wildlife, including various bird species and mammals like wild boar and foxes, especially in quieter areas.

Q: What is the best time of year to go urban hiking in Paratge Natural Municipal La Murta?
A: Paratge Natural Municipal La Murta benefits from a unique microclimate with mild temperatures and high humidity, making it suitable for hiking throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn are particularly pleasant, with comfortable temperatures and vibrant natural colors. The Senda Botánica's appearance changes throughout the seasons, offering a different experience each time.
tips:
This cylindrical construction was used to store the snow that fell in winter or the snow transported from the Sierra Mariola and which was transported with cars.
It was a very common practice in the 16th and 18th centuries and was used to make ice cream and soft drinks, preserve food, and even make medicines, reduce the
fever and relieve bruises. The "snow house" is a large well, very deep, and with a cover to avoid sunstroke. The top protrudes above
of! ground level and the rest is buried, thus helping the thermal insulation necessary to preserve the snow. The interior walls were made of masonry. Between
On the upper level and on the roof there are several openings through which you could enter inside and load the snow, then they were covered to maintain the temperature. With wood
"They made steps to go down to the lower levels. At the lowest part there was a fountain or simply a gutter to let the accumulated water out.
After the snowfall, the well was filled with snow. Inside it was crushed, as much as possible, with the feet and with wooden pillars, to turn it into
ice. This operation lasted a short time to avoid frostbite on the feet. At a certain height, more or less 1 meter, rice straw was piled up and on top again
snow. When the well was full, the snow was left to settle, covered, and monitored to prevent theft.
The ice was cut into blocks to be extracted and placed in wooden boxes wrapped with straw to avoid, as much as possible, temperature loss. As
They used animals for transportation, these were wrapped with blankets. The journey was made at night and quickly. Despite doing so, the load was reduced
coming soon. This refrigerator was for private use of the monastery.

Sieh dir die Gegend und my Wanderung im Video an: https://youtu.be/jKT0SaA3j6g

According to the writings found in the monastery itself, its origins date back to the 14th century, when the lord of the Murta lands, Arnau de Serra, decided to donate them to a group of hermits who had settled in the area. His only condition was that a religious community be founded that lived under the rule of Saint Jerome.
